title: Podman machine init on Windows spams chmod access errors
body:
### Issue Description

`Podman machine init` on Windows spams chmod access errors

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install podman on Windows 10
2. Run `podman machine init`
3. Check the output


### Describe the results you received

podman machine init
Extracting compressed file
Importing operating system into WSL (this may take a few minutes on a new WSL install)...
Import wird ausgeführt. Dieser Vorgang kann einige Minuten dauern.
Der Vorgang wurde erfolgreich beendet.
chown: cannot access '/usr/share/doc/shadow-utils': No such file or directory
[...] // hundreds more chown / chmod errors
Configuring system...
Generating public/private ed25519 key pair.
Your identification has been saved in podman-machine-default
Your public key has been saved in podman-machine-default.pub
The key fingerprint is:
SHA256:GWgB+p/HlM99Nj5ogKvAQjvG0hA2JdAbImGhoFafZkI root@LAPTOP-I0Q0R94D
The key's randomart image is:
+--[ED25519 256]--+
|=*.E...          |
|B B.. .o         |
|==.+ =o .        |
|o.o.+.   +       |
|. . .   S.       |
| = o . +.o..     |
|. B o o o.o...+  |
| o o . ..   o+.. |
|      ..   .  .. |
+----[SHA256]-----+
Machine init complete
To start your machine run:

        podman machine start

### Describe the results you expected

Only output about a successful initialization.

Thanks for the report. These failures issues are harmless (but I agree, annoying) and are a temporary side effect of multi-step update to move to Fedora 37.  A fix related to rpm restore with changes to rpms impl in f37 was introduced in the 4.4.3 client.  This fix leads to the warnings in f36 (associated with not installed files) that aren't present in f37's rpm version. I just moved the latest image forward to f37 so now this issue should be resolved. If you rerun the init command you should see v37.0.1 pulled down with no warnings produced. Feel free to reopen if you still see issues.
